finding: `next --claim` freezes on a CID-name collision (`insufficient-mutation-intent`) even when a valid `atm.brokerConflictResolution.v1` artifact authorizes the pair, and the requiredCommand it prints (team broker resolve) therefore cannot unblock the claim.
expectedBehavior: Claim admission must consume matching broker conflict resolution artifacts the same way the governed commit lane does (`readResolutionAuthorizedForeignTaskIds`).
evidenceOrRepro: TASK-AAO-FABLE-004 vs TASK-AAO-FABLE-005: no common writable file, resolution artifact BCR-nvfv9w recorded, claim still froze.
followUp: Fixed in TASK-AAO-0200: extracted shared `readResolutionAuthorizedForeignTaskIds` reader, claim admission consumes matching BCR sidecars before freezing; regression `packages/cli/src/commands/next/__tests__/claim-broker-resolution.spec.ts`.
